title: Global Monitoring — Push Template
short_description: Pub/sub push template: a cron reads this tenant's own status and pushes it to the global-monitoring dashboard. Self-serve — required config is just a read token, the tenant's API URL, and a label; share-tier is optional and cadence is the cron schedule.
release_notes: v0.8.0: fixes from first-tenant feedback. (1) Use the public-invoke URL for the collect endpoint (the /api/v1 path 401'd unauthenticated). (2) Cadence is now the cron schedule itself (default every 5 min) — removed the fragile interval-gating + state table. (3) Payload carries a sender-side pushed_at (epoch ms UTC). (4) All globals prefixed gm_ to avoid collisions. (5) Added run logging. (6) More resilient error handling (a failing window no longer sinks the push). (7) Removed customer-specific examples from the setup page. v0.8.1: read globals defensively — context.globals.get() throws on a missing global, so the optional gm_tier crashed the run; now guarded (default T3). Verified end-to-end on FDE (scheduled run, clean self-read, landed on the hub). v0.8.2: the run now logs a per-window summary of exactly what was pushed (interactions/open_issues/active_alerts/#business_metrics/#agents/#errors) and includes it in the return output, so users can verify the sent data in the function's run logs on the source tenant. v0.8.3: two robustness fixes from tenant feedback. (1) gm_self_api_url is now normalized — you can paste whatever is in the browser address bar: the dashboard host (.app) is auto-rewritten to the API host (.api), a missing scheme is added, and any pasted path is dropped. A correct .api URL is unchanged. (2) The run now fails honestly: if the self-read errored on every window (no real data), the execution is reported as failed instead of a misleading 'success' just because the downstream push POST returned 200 — it still pushes the error snapshot so the tenant's trouble shows on the dashboard. Every failure path now throws (missing config, transport error, rejected push, total self-read failure) instead of returning a soft error object — errors are surfaced, never swallowed into a green run. v0.8.4: full error visibility. (1) The run now surfaces the exact read-error MESSAGES (e.g. 'business_metrics: HTTP 403: …') in the function's Output (return `errors` array) and Logs, not just a count. (2) ANY read error fails the run (red) — a green success now means every read succeeded, not merely that the POST returned 200; partial data is still pushed so the dashboard shows what it can, and the thrown error names the failed reads plus a likely-cause hint (e.g. missing metrics:view). (3) Severity honesty (shared core): a failed health read (issues/alerts) can no longer report 'healthy' — it downgrades to 'attention' so a blind read never shows green.

required_secrets.name: SERVICE_MONITORING_TOKEN
required_secrets.description: Bearer token for reading this same tenant's own communications/issues/alerts before pushing the summary out. Sent as Authorization: Bearer. Legacy API Key values are still tolerated by the code.

required_global_variables.name: gm_self_api_url
required_global_variables.display_name: This tenant's own API base URL
required_global_variables.description: This tenant's own API base URL, used for the self-read. Pattern: https://<tenant>.api.<env>.wonderful.ai

required_global_variables.name: gm_tenant_label
required_global_variables.description: Display name this tenant is shown as on the dashboard. Not hardcoded in code so the same plugin can be installed on multiple tenants.

required_global_variables.name: gm_tier
required_global_variables.description: How much detail leaves this tenant: T0 (name+severity), T1 (+health rollup), T2 (+per-agent KPIs, business metrics, monitor rollups), T3 (+full alert/monitor lists). Optional — defaults to T3 (full).
